forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: f580
+project: spatial-nav
+title: Replace TS sneak-code port with fixture-file or property-style cross-language check
+---
+## What
+
+The TS port of `generate_sneak_codes` in `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx` (`generateSneakCodesPort`, around the `SNEAK_ALPHABET` constant) is a hand-written re-implementation of `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/sneak.rs`. The two implementations have no compile-time link, so a future change to either side (alphabet ordering, `pick_two_letter_prefix_count` formula, single -> two-letter fill order) can drift silently — the overlay would dispatch focus to the wrong scope at runtime without any test catching it.
+
+This task replaces the hand-port with one of:
+
+(a) **Fixture file approach** — emit a `sneak-fixture.json` from a Rust `#[test]` in `swissarmyhammer-focus` (e.g. `tests/sneak_fixture.rs`) that runs `generate_sneak_codes(n)` over a representative count set and writes the result. The TS test reads the fixture and asserts the TS port produces the same output. Either keep both sides and assert equality, or drop the TS port and have the test consume the fixture directly.
+
+(b) **Property-style test** — exercise the TS port across `[1, 5, 23, 24, 50, 200, 529]` and assert shape invariants:
+- length matches `count`
+- all codes distinct
+- prefix-free (no code is a prefix of another)
+- alphabet only contains the 23 letters in `SNEAK_ALPHABET`
+- single-letter codes appear before two-letter codes
+- single-letter codes are in alphabet order
+Add a Rust counterpart that asserts the same invariants, so any future divergence in either side surfaces as a test failure.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] Either a fixture file is emitted by Rust and consumed by the TS test, OR both sides assert the same property invariants.
+- [ ] The drift-risk comment block in `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx` (the `SOURCE OF TRUTH` / `DRIFT RISK` / `FOLLOW-UP` block above `SNEAK_ALPHABET`) is updated to reflect the chosen approach, or removed if no longer relevant.
+- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test spatial-nav-jump-to` still passes 7/7.
+- [ ] If the fixture approach is taken: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus sneak_fixture` (or equivalent) passes and produces the fixture file deterministically.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test spatial-nav-jump-to` — passes.
+- [ ] If fixture path is chosen: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` — passes.
+
+## Background
+
+This is a follow-up nit raised in the review of task `01KQYWWQ0C4E9AFM942BMJHMFX` (End-to-end spatial test for Jump-To overlay). The reviewer accepted the hand-port for landing that task but flagged the drift risk explicitly. See the comment block in the spatial-nav-jump-to test file for the full context. #nav-jump
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Replace TS sneak-code port with fixture-file or property-style cross-language check

## What

The TS port of `generate_sneak_codes` in `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx` (`generateSneakCodesPort`, around the `SNEAK_ALPHABET` constant) is a hand-written re-implementation of `swissarmyhammer-focus/src/sneak.rs`. The two implementations have no compile-time link, so a future change to either side (alphabet ordering, `pick_two_letter_prefix_count` formula, single -> two-letter fill order) can drift silently — the overlay would dispatch focus to the wrong scope at runtime without any test catching it.

This task replaces the hand-port with one of:

(a) **Fixture file approach** — emit a `sneak-fixture.json` from a Rust `#[test]` in `swissarmyhammer-focus` (e.g. `tests/sneak_fixture.rs`) that runs `generate_sneak_codes(n)` over a representative count set and writes the result. The TS test reads the fixture and asserts the TS port produces the same output. Either keep both sides and assert equality, or drop the TS port and have the test consume the fixture directly.

(b) **Property-style test** — exercise the TS port across `[1, 5, 23, 24, 50, 200, 529]` and assert shape invariants:
- length matches `count`
- all codes distinct
- prefix-free (no code is a prefix of another)
- alphabet only contains the 23 letters in `SNEAK_ALPHABET`
- single-letter codes appear before two-letter codes
- single-letter codes are in alphabet order
Add a Rust counterpart that asserts the same invariants, so any future divergence in either side surfaces as a test failure.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Either a fixture file is emitted by Rust and consumed by the TS test, OR both sides assert the same property invariants.
- [ ] The drift-risk comment block in `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx` (the `SOURCE OF TRUTH` / `DRIFT RISK` / `FOLLOW-UP` block above `SNEAK_ALPHABET`) is updated to reflect the chosen approach, or removed if no longer relevant.
- [ ] `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test spatial-nav-jump-to` still passes 7/7.
- [ ] If the fixture approach is taken: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus sneak_fixture` (or equivalent) passes and produces the fixture file deterministically.

## Tests

- [ ] Run `cd kanban-app/ui && pnpm test spatial-nav-jump-to` — passes.
- [ ] If fixture path is chosen: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus` — passes.

## Background

This is a follow-up nit raised in the review of task `01KQYWWQ0C4E9AFM942BMJHMFX` (End-to-end spatial test for Jump-To overlay). The reviewer accepted the hand-port for landing that task but flagged the drift risk explicitly. See the comment block in the spatial-nav-jump-to test file for the full context. #nav-jump

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -37,4 +37,13 @@

 ## Background

-This is a follow-up nit raised in the review of task `01KQYWWQ0C4E9AFM942BMJHMFX` (End-to-end spatial test for Jump-To overlay). The reviewer accepted the hand-port for landing that task but flagged the drift risk explicitly. See the comment block in the spatial-nav-jump-to test file for the full context. #nav-jump
\ No newline at end of file
+This is a follow-up nit raised in the review of task `01KQYWWQ0C4E9AFM942BMJHMFX` (End-to-end spatial test for Jump-To overlay). The reviewer accepted the hand-port for landing that task but flagged the drift risk explicitly. See the comment block in the spatial-nav-jump-to test file for the full context. #nav-jump
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-08 15:38)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/sneak_fixture.rs:97-127` — A bare `#[test]` always writes to `kanban-app/ui/src/test/fixtures/sneak-fixture.json` whenever `cargo test` is run. That means a developer running the workspace test suite locally with a clean working tree can end up with a dirty tree that they didn't intend to modify (e.g. cross-platform line-ending differences, an in-flight `git stash`, or a partially staged commit). Why it matters: tests should be observers, not mutators of source-controlled files. Suggested fix: gate the write behind an env var (`SAH_BLESS_FIXTURE=1` or `BLESS=1`) and have the un-blessed path read the file and assert its contents match what `generate_sneak_codes` would produce — that turns the same test into a verifier in normal runs and a regenerator on demand. Bonus: it removes the dependency on a separate CI `git diff --exit-code` step, since the un-blessed test fails directly when the fixture is stale.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-focus/tests/sneak_fixture.rs:18-23` — Drift detection is documented as "CI runs the test and then checks the working tree is clean (`git diff --exit-code`)". This PR does not include or modify any CI workflow that enforces that gate. Why it matters: without the gate the fixture can land out-of-sync with the algorithm and no test catches it — the whole drift-prevention story rests on a check that may not exist. Suggested fix: either (a) add a CI step that runs `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-focus --test sneak_fixture` followed by `git diff --exit-code -- kanban-app/ui/src/test/fixtures/sneak-fixture.json`, or (b) adopt the BLESS-gated pattern from the previous finding so the test itself fails on drift without needing CI plumbing.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `kanban-app/ui/src/spatial-nav-jump-to.spatial.test.tsx:300-303` — `lookupSneakCodes((a.count as number) ?? 0)` falls back to count=0 when `args.count` is missing. The fixture has `"0": []` so this returns an empty array silently. If a future refactor accidentally drops the `count` arg from the production `generate_jump_codes` call, the test would still pass against an empty code list rather than failing loudly. Suggested fix: throw if `count` is missing/non-numeric — the IPC contract requires it, and an explicit failure here is more useful than a silent empty.
\ No newline at end of file
